pattern: The game never needs to be replayed on a real array: tracking only the current champion and its consecutive win streak reproduces the same outcome in one pass.
hints: You never need to rebuild or rotate the array; just track who the current champion is and how many rounds in a row it has won.
Walk the array from the second element onward, comparing each challenger to the champion: a bigger challenger becomes the new champion with a streak of one, otherwise the streak grows by one.

Stop as soon as the streak reaches k. If it never does, the array runs out first, which only happens once the true maximum has already become champion, so the last champion tracked is still the answer.

edge_inputs.description: champion changes hands a couple of times before the streak is met
streak requirement equal to array length minus one
k far larger than needed still lands on the global maximum
the very first element is already the maximum
k of one just needs the first round's winner

stress.description: large distinct random array with a large win-streak target
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100000))
    arr = rng.sample(range(1, 1000000), n)
    k = rng.randint(1, 10 ** 9)
    return [arr, k]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def getWinner(self, arr, k):
        from collections import deque
        dq = deque(arr)
        champion = None
        streak = 0
        rounds = 0
        max_rounds = len(arr)
        while rounds < max_rounds and streak < k:
            a = dq.popleft()
            b = dq.popleft()
            if a > b:
                winner, loser = a, b
            else:
                winner, loser = b, a
            dq.appendleft(winner)
            dq.append(loser)
            if winner == champion:
                streak += 1
            else:
                champion = winner
                streak = 1
            rounds += 1
        return champion
